48-Hour Permit Triage Packet Sample Report — LADBS Permit Delay / Supplemental Review Case

A client-ready research and organization packet for teams trying to understand where a permit is stuck, what should be confirmed next, and how to focus the next follow-up.

Fictional sample case for demonstration only. No actual permit issue is implied at this address. PermitPulse is an independent permit research and organization service. We are not affiliated with LADBS or any government agency.
Project Snapshot 01
Project Name Chinatown Tenant Improvement
Address 726 N Spring Street, Los Angeles, CA 90012
Project Type Tenant Improvement / Interior Remodel
Primary Concern Supplemental review appears stalled after correction response resubmittal
Prepared For Sample Architect / Owner Team
Prepared By PermitPulse — Independent Permit Research & Organization Service
Packet Includes ✓
  • Executive situation summary
  • Record trail and follow-up timeline
  • Likely bottleneck diagnosis
  • Missing clarity checklist
  • Questions for LADBS / reviewer
  • Escalation-ready client summary
Current Situation

The project appears delayed after supplemental resubmittal. Public-facing status does not clearly explain whether the main bottleneck is reassignment, correction-response review, or an outside clearance dependency.

Most Likely Bottleneck

Delayed supplemental review assignment combined with unclear written confirmation of the exact next step.

Recommended Immediate Actions
  • Confirm current assignment / active reviewer status
  • Verify whether an outside department or clearance is blocking progress
  • Review whether correction responses clearly address prior comments
  • Prepare a concise follow-up summary before additional resubmittal effort
Risk If Unresolved

Further delay, duplicated effort, unclear owner expectations, and additional time lost chasing the wrong issue.

Why This Matters

When permit status is unclear, teams often lose time chasing the wrong issue. This packet organizes the record trail so the next call, email, or meeting is more productive.

Fictional sample case for demonstration only. No actual permit issue is implied at this address. Page 01 / 03
Record Trail + Diagnosis

Where the file appears to be stuck

Sample Report

Project Timeline / Record Trail

May 2 Initial submittal
May 16 Correction comments issued
May 23 Correction response / supplemental package submitted
May 30 No visible progress update
June 4 Follow-up email sent
June 10 Still awaiting clear assignment / review movement

Bottleneck Diagnosis

Category Observable Fact Why It Matters Triage Read
Supplemental assignment Resubmittal appears on May 23, but no visible review movement by June 10. That gap suggests the file may be waiting on assignment or queue movement. Most likely bottleneck
Correction response clarity Correction comments were issued May 16, but there is no public indication that the response was accepted as sufficient. Even after resubmittal, unresolved comments can quietly stall progress. Needs confirmation
Outside clearance dependency Public-facing status does not clearly show whether another department is holding progress. Hidden dependencies often cause applicants to chase the wrong issue. Possible secondary blocker
Applicant visibility The portal view does not clearly translate the actual next step. Lack of clarity creates wasted follow-up and client confusion. Communication gap
Document completeness The visible record suggests the package is assembled enough to move forward. This makes outright missing-submittal failure less likely than workflow or assignment friction. Less likely primary issue

What May Be Causing the Delay

  • Supplemental review may not yet be actively assigned
  • Correction response may not fully resolve all prior comments
  • Another department or clearance may be quietly holding progress
  • Public status may not reflect the internal workflow in a useful way

Agency Dependency Map

Applicant / Architect
→
LADBS Plan Check
→
Supplemental Review Queue
Possible Branches / Dependencies
Planning
Fire / Life Safety
Public Works
LADWP / utility-related
Other required clearances
Reviewer clarification
Observed progress

Initial submittal completed; correction comments issued; supplemental package resubmitted.

Where visibility weakens

No clear signal of reassignment, accepted responses, or outside clearance status.

Best next clarification

Confirm whether the file is actively assigned, and whether any outside clearance is currently preventing progress.

PermitPulse is an independent permit research and organization service. We are not affiliated with LADBS or any government agency. Page 02 / 03
Action Plan + Escalation Packet

Questions, next steps, and copy-ready summary

Client-ready format

Questions to Ask LADBS / Reviewer

  • Has the supplemental package been assigned for active review?
  • Are any correction responses still considered incomplete or unclear?
  • Is another department or clearance currently preventing movement?
  • Would a preliminary plan check or clarification meeting help resolve this faster?
  • What single issue should be resolved first to move this permit forward?

Recommended Next-Step Plan

Within 24 Hours
  • Send a concise follow-up summary with timeline
  • Confirm assignment / review status
  • Clarify whether any outside clearance is involved
Within 48 Hours
  • Resolve open questions
  • Prepare escalation-ready summary if needed
  • Decide whether to request preliminary plan check / code clarification support

Escalation-Ready Summary

Escalation Draft

This supplemental package was resubmitted on May 23, and as of June 10 there is still no clear indication of active review movement or confirmed assignment. At this stage, the team needs immediate clarification on whether the file is sitting in queue, whether any correction-response issue remains unresolved, and whether another clearance is silently holding progress. Without that clarity, additional time will be lost pursuing the wrong next step while schedule pressure and client uncertainty continue to grow.

PermitPulse organizes the public record, timeline, open questions, and next-step logic to help project teams follow up more clearly. PermitPulse does not replace the architect, engineer, expediter, or agency reviewer.

Fictional sample case for demonstration only. No actual permit issue is implied at this address.
